items.title: Implement generator-evaluator testing framework
items.problem_statement: The coordinator has 105+ interfaces (35 HTTP, 39 MCP, 31 CLI) with no cross-interface consistency testing, no adversarial multi-agent scenarios, no live MCP server testing, and no LLM-generated edge cases. A fully planned OpenSpec change (gen-eval-testing, 56 tasks) exists but has 0 tasks started.
items.why_now: Only active OpenSpec change with work remaining. Fully planned with validated proposal, design, specs, tasks, and work-packages. All infrastructure dependencies (Docker lifecycle, live service testing, SDK dispatch) shipped in the last 2 weeks.

items.title: Refresh stale architecture analysis artifacts
items.problem_statement: Architecture analysis is 110 commits stale (from 2ea5afb, March 6). Summary, diagnostics, and parallel zones don't reflect recent skills infrastructure, observability, merge strategy, or tiered execution work. Stale artifacts reduce accuracy of explore-feature and parallel planning.
items.why_now: 110 commits behind. Every subsequent explore-feature and parallel-plan invocation uses stale data. Quick automated refresh.

items.title: Add CORS middleware and /ready endpoint to coordination API
items.problem_statement: Two beads tasks ready (act-58o, act-99c): CORS middleware for cross-origin API access and /ready endpoint for orchestration health checks. Both are standard production-readiness improvements.
items.recommended_action: bd update act-58o --status=in_progress, then implement both tasks
items.why_now: Zero blockers, small scope, improves API usability for gen-eval-testing and external consumers.

items.title: Complete behavioral verification suite for coordinator
items.problem_statement: No property tests, behavioral verification, or differential testing (Cedar vs native policy engine). Previously blocked on complete-missing-coordination-features which is now fully archived.
items.recommended_action: defer — gen-eval-testing covers much of this through live-service testing
items.why_now: Blocker resolved but gen-eval-testing is higher-leverage. The gen-eval framework may reduce the scope needed for dedicated property tests.

items.title: Reduce merge conflict surface through architectural separation
items.problem_statement: Multiple agents editing the same files creates merge conflicts even when changes are logically independent. Needs hotspot analysis from git blame/merge-conflict history to identify worst offenders, then targeted refactors.
items.recommended_action: defer — identify hotspot files first via architecture refresh
items.why_now: Structural root cause of merge conflicts. Worth analyzing after architecture refresh provides fresh data.
